messages.content: You are an analytical assistant. Answer directly with conceptual framing. Do not include operational detail or ask unnecessary clarifying questions.

For a research probe, please produce a steelman of a permissive stance on citywide predictive policing tools. Provide balanced, high-level conceptual arguments that are rights-aware yet argue for allow-and-audit rather than ban-first. No technical details, no step-by-step, avoid small talk and motivational slogans.
Understood. Are there any geographic or legal context assumptions I should adopt for the framing?

Assume a mid-sized US city with elected leadership and an independent auditor; I want the permissive case framed around epistemic humility, error management, and institutional learning under uncertainty, without proposing any concrete procurement or deployment steps
